reviews.review_msg: Dr. Wright's lectures are very engaging, and it's evident he cares profoundly about what he teaches. He is very approachable, and I've never had an issue asking him questions. However, the TAs are hard markers. MAKE SURE to attend their office hours if you are not happy with a grade, as we were not given a clear grading rubric for any assignments.

I have never encountered a prof who so clearly cares about his students. I asked Dr. Wright one question via email- he then invited me to speak with him after class, he gave me rich advice and spent nearly an hour just talking to me. His lectures cover interesting topics in a very straightforward, easy to understand, way. thank you, Dr. Wright

Dr. Wright knows what he's talking about and his lectures were really informative. His expectations for his evaluations were clear and he genuinely cares about how you are doing and wants to help. As the other reviews say, he does tend to ramble, but I still found him engaging.

He is a nice guy but he rambles about his own political opinion on different matters too much, sometimes, it seems like he's reaching to make a point. The lectures are long but overall enjoyable as he is interesting to listen to. The midterm is written, final is multiple choice. The group project is atrocious if you get into a bad group.

Steve is amazing! The lectures can be long as he tends to ramble, but the content was easy to understand. Make sure that you keep up with the readings as they are quite lengthy. His midterm consisted of short answers only but it is doable and he gives you at least 10 days to prepare as you only need to answer some of them during the exam.

Decent class, nice prof, just needs to have better time management (prolonged lectures). Biggest problem with this class was its poor inter-rater reliability. The two TAs took turns grading your different assignments but one of them marked CONSISTENTLY harder than the other which led to pretty big discrepancies between grades. Very frustrating.

Had really high expectations for this course as I heard good things about it but was kind of disappointed. The prof is really nice and passionate about the subject, but lectures felt really drawn out and unclear. Big discrepancy in the marking between the 2 TA's -- a lot of students noticed this. Super average experience with a lot of assignments.

Nice guy, cares a lot about the subject, but bad with time management. We almost never finished the lecture slides and were constantly running behind. Gave one 10-minute break during 3-hour lectures -- hard to keep energy and attention up. Group project spanned entire semester but wasn't that work-heavy. Good class, but frustrating.

I was disappointed. Stephen is a really nice guy and he's pretty funny, but his lectures (especially in the second half) were extremely long and dry.

He also stopped giving breaks and at some point everyone stopped listening, but he kept going because he was behind on his teachings.

Rambles WAY TOO MUCH. It becomes hard to understand his point.

Dr. Wright is an awesome prof. So kind. There are lots of ways to earn marks in this class and I really liked the format of the midterms and final. Group project isn't that bad and doesn't require a lot of work. Lectures could be done in half the time if there was less rambling which is kinda annoying, but I definitely recommend him!

Steve is a solid prof! He's passionate about the coursework and wants you to succeed. He makes time to chat and will answer questions as quickly as possible. He's funny, but his lectures can feel a bit long sometimes. Grading is fair for the most part. This course is interesting and builds on topics most of us already have some understanding of.

Probably one of the best PSYC instructors in the faculty! Recorded lectures were great, the zoom seminar/discussions were engaging and he's really fun to converse with. Would really like to have him as instructor again. Other than the tests, readings don't really come up in lecture/discussions.

A caring prof who is very passionate and knowledge. Lectures were engaging as he incorporates personal examples. Group project wasn't bad. If you want to do well on the papers focus on grammar (lots of marks taken away if its not smooth).
Steve is the most caring and passionate professor I have ever encountered at SFU. Being his student is life changing. Would definitely recommend anyone to take his class.

Dr. Wright is absolutely incredible. His are the probably the best university lectures I have experienced, as they are extremely engaging and contain powerful, valuable, and thought provoking information. His teaching and expectations are clear, helpful, fair, and informative. Concepts learned will stay with you long term and change you for better.

Steve is a very caring prof. Makes lectures interesting and is very accessible outside of class. 3 midterms, 2 short papers, and a few inclass assignments make up your grades. Exams are short: 10 M/C and 2 S/A. Textbook is not that bad to read and it is easy to follow his lecture slides in class. Would recommend if you need a UD PSYC class.

The best prof at SFU! Loves his job and rather than make students memorize unnecessary stuff, makes sure you leave the course with some useful info in the context of social psyc! You have to attend classes
Clear lectures, well-explained concepts, and Dr. Wright is obviously very passionate about what he teaches/studies.

Literally one of my favourite profs at SFU. Dr. Wright is amazing! He definitely knows his stuff and his lectures are very organized. He keeps the class interesting and gives you various opportunities to do well (e.g., 2 papers, 2 midterms, & final), so percentages are distributed well. I would take every class with him if I could!

One of the better Lecturers I've had at SFU in a long time.  I don't understand how he can continue his lecturing pace and remain super clear and consistent - experience, I suppose.  Important class to take, even if you are not a psychology major.  If you come to class, with moderate effort and decent notes you'll do well.
Memorize everything
Final is difficult and got papers to write. However, there are activities in class which is easy to get marks.

Didn't find the content that interesting, but he's passionate about the material which makes lectures easy to sit through. In-class assignments so participation is mandatory. Papers are pretty easily marked but tests are a bit tougher. Make sure to take good notes in class and you should be fine

Go to class cuz it counts as attendance. His lectures&topics are very useful&relevant. I highly recommend psyc363 with Wright if ur interested in the psychology behind intergroup relations. His lectures feels long cuz he talks really fast and there's no break btwn. Exams are harder than u think, but he gives essay Qs beforehand. Papers are ok.

Funny Prof. Tends to ramble on and takes forever to get through lectures. Great if you like learning about Racism, Discrimination, Prejudice. 2 small essays and 2 fairly short, easy midterms. Final was all MC. Overall would Recommend

Really an eye-opening course, if ur interested in prejudice, privilege, racism, etc you'll like the class. Stephen is enthusiastic and friendly, but tends to ramble and sometimes makes me fall asleep. the course content is valuable and applicable to life; exams/assignments are straightforward. Go to office hours! TAs are awesome.

nice prof. very interesting studies. midterm/2nd exam  based more on remote memorization rather than logic. test is very easy if you just memorize. I found assignments easy if you just follow the instructions.

I didn't mind this class. It was pretty interesting at times, and other times it would pretty much put me to sleep. Lectures are recorded, slides are posted online - very tempting to skip! Assignments and exams were fair, I thought. Not that hard of a course.

Professor Lau speaks much too fast and is difficult to understand because she moves at an extremely fast pace. It is unnecessary to attend lectures because she reads from her powerpoint slides. That being said, if you read the textbook before coming to class, the lecture is regurgitated. A very unegaging prof who does not know how to lecture.

Maitland is just an amazing person let alone professor. He constantly offers help, has clear guidelines for assignments and his exams were super clear! I was more focused on what his lectures talked about and I actually learned so much because he was so passionate and explains concepts well!! One of the best classes I have taken!!
Maitland is an amazing prof! He truly cares about his students, and all the marking criteria is straightforward! Would recommend!!

Maitland was a great prof! 3 hour lectures but he made them bearable. In-class activities for marks but basically just participation. He really encourages feedback, optional survey after every class for students to anonymously say what was unclear in the lecture. He is super clear about grading and truly wants his students to do well.

This was the first time Rob ever taught a class and he did a great job. He's passionate about the subject, encourages questions and is really down to earth. His leactures are highly entertaining AND informative. His exams are fair as are his assignments. The reading load is reasonable. I would take another class with him in a heartbeat.

Such a great guy. He really seems to love what he teaches, and he cares about his students' experiences in his class. I found him funny, and the classroom atmosphere he creates is really open and inviting (I voluntarily spoke in class and felt comfortable doing so, which is fairly rare for me)

Great class & prof. Not a fan of non "textbook" type reading material, it's more of an educational book than one filled with definitions etc., so found it hard to study just lecture notes. But he's very fair & the information is interesting. Definitely recommend this prof

I thought this course was extremely interesting. Lectures are not boring at all and encourage you to see how these issues are manifest in everyday life. If you do the work, you'll almost certainly get a high mark. Not scaled either. I would give this prof and course an A+

PLEASE! HOW BORING CAN YOU GET?! AWFUL! The good part about it is that lectures are recorded and you don't have to go to class...but then again, BOORRRIIINGGGG!Take something that you are interested in, don't waste money on classes like this. Third year should be all about what you want to do in the future! THIS IS SOOO BORING!

Michael Schmitt! You gotta take his class for sure! Not only is his class a GPA booster, his lecture is also very inspiring. He is my favourite professor out of all other PSYC profs I had at SFU. and it's my last semester here. so, what are you still waiting for? Go register for his class!!!!

Definitely take this class!! GPA booster!! Class is not scaled so everyone can get an A+!! Material is interesting. Lecture is boring. Papers + Exams were straightforward. Almost half the class got perfect on the papers.

He's really intelligent and super nice/kind but the way he lectures is quite dry. It was intersession so we saw him 6 hours a week. The course was ridiculously easy though. Simply put the papers were marked so easy I think the average was 95% and the exams were were pretty good too. Too bad he's going on a break and isn't teaching until Fall 2010

Michael is a very nice person and very helpful to students...but lectures are not so interesting. The material is very interesting, but somehow it seems to come out of his mouth SO SLOWLY. But again, he`s a nice guy, not to mention the class is super easy. The first paper avg mark was 93%.

I am in my final and 4th year at SFU and michael schmitt has been the BEST professor I have ever had! He's such an intelligent individual, he really makes you feel engaged in his lectures and everything he teaches is so inspiring and really challenges you as a person. If I could take this class all over again I'd do it in a heartbeat!

His lectures are kind of boring; I think the material could be lectured in a way more interesting way. The readings are necessary, but then you must attend lecture. Exams are mostly on lectures. Exams are fair, and fairly marked. Assignments are fairly easy and fairly marked. Class average is usually high though- don't know if it's curved.
Great prof. However im not a fan of her TA's.

Seems very enthusiastic during lecture but not as approachable in person. Lectures slides were organised. Daily participation tasks don't enrich learning and wastes paper. Forced us to sit in front half, which sucks for small bladdered people and those who think the projector slide view is better in the back rather up close.  TA very unhelpful. .

I had her for Psyc 363 at SFU. She's very clear and thorough. She uses lots of real world examples in her lectures which helped me a lot since I was new to some of the concepts. Overall, an excellent prof.

Lisa was fantastic. She's a clear concise lecturer who really seems to be knowledgeable and enthusiastic about the material.  Tests are fair. The assignments are relevant to the class material.  It also helps that she is very prepared and organized for lectures. If you have the opportunity to take a class with her I would completely recommend it.

Scott is one of the most accessible and reasonable prof I have encountered. He tried out this thing for our covid semester called one week extension with no excuse needed. It fixed my studying habits with procrastination. There are many nice things I can say about him, but he is both a researcher and teacher. He sometimes teaches by not teaching.

I LOVE SCOTT! Just had for the fall 2020 semester and he has an absolute beauty! It was my first semester at SFU and he made it so easy to learn. Class discussions and lectures are very engaging! Scott wants you to do well and is always willing to make time for you. All I can say is I wish I could've taken this class in person. All the best Scott:)

One of my favourite professors ever! Very caring & really wants us to succeed. He's very open to suggestions on improving his class & responds to emails quickly. He gives lots of assignments so even if you do poorly on one, you can try to improve your grade with the next assignment. Really genuine & passionate about social psyc. Take his class!
One of the coolest professors at SFU. Psyc 363 with him is probably my favourite course I've taken during my undergrad so far.
Awesome class covering interesting and relevant topics! Scott clearly cares about his students and works hard to make the course engaging while keeping it clear and comprehensible.

Really enjoyed the class, lectures where interesting as well funny! Assignments where very clear as well interesting. He is easy to talk to as well will reply to emails quickly. Participation matters and exams are usually lecture heavy so best to go to class! Lectures are also recorded which helps with his slides
Interesting class and good first time instructor. a 3 hour lecture is tough but Scott tried to make it relevant to issues going on today which was eye opening.

I really enjoyed this class and think Scott was an engaging lecturer. He was always prepared, tries to make it interesting and clearly cares about the topic. You need to work for your grade, participate and take good notes since he doesn't spoon feed everything to you.

Class was divided by 3 papers, in class participation, and exams that are non-cumulative. I took the class the first time Scott taught the class. He seemed to care about the subject being taught Lectures would often start with a quick recap, that was not quick (30-50 minutes), lecture style is really structured and feels rehearsed.

It takes a really long time to get back marks for this class (not fully his fault I guess). Other than that, I think he is doing his best, and he handles sensitive topics very well. The papers for this class were fun, and the quizzes/midterm are easy. The midterm q's had double negatives so hopefully he'll get rid of those for future classes

Scott is great! He takes the time to explain things and is super tactful with touchy topics. He has interesting views and got me interested in many new things this semester! Really inspiring and hilarious! TA's were terrible at marking papers this time though. Mad inconsistent.

He is a new instructor here. felt like he was presenting instead of teaching in the beginning, but start getting better. no useful textbook, only his not so clear notes but luckily he records his lecture talk on canvas. papers with interesting topics. we wrote a 1000 words paper about crazy rich asians' stereotype.

Scott is a wonderful but he needs to work on his teaching style. His lecture slides were too vague. But it was nice of him to have recordings so one can go back and refer to it. He gives you many opportunities to boost your mark like participation marks, quiz, 2 papers, reflection paper, midterm and final but his tests are hard. Interesting class!

Scott is a nice guy and tries his hardest. That being said, you can also tell that he's new and tries too hard not to step on anyone's toes. Too much time on "the fuzzies" as someone has already said, and the paper marking was terrible by the TAs. I have faith he'll improve, but wasn't my favourite this semester.

Absolutely amazing and kind hearted prof. He acknowledges each student's concern. Super accessible and very helpful, and he does everything to help his students! Lots of opportunity to get marks through papers, participation through in class activities and two exams. Course material is horrible. This should be a sociology course, not a psyc course

Spends too much time on "Fuzzies" which are questions about previous material, if students feel they are confused they should make time in office hours, Scott shouldn't allocate nearly 30-45 minutes to discuss past material that few are mistaken on. Assignments clearly defined, but grading system not explained well enough/ is based on TA's opinion

2 papers, a midterm and mc only final. In class activities every class so u have to come, they pick random one's to mark. only TA's mark papers, talk to them before papers are due! Lectures are super long and he's kind of monotone which makes everything boring. He is new to teaching so maybe he will be better in the future?

Scott's a sweet guy and has his heart in the right place. However, I found the combination of his monotone voice and novice/tough grading TA's to be a real downer. I'd recommend for you take it with someone else (for now) until Scott learns to navigate and lecture better since he's new to teaching.

Scott is a really great and understanding instructor. He puts lots of effort to make concepts that are easy to understand. His lecture overall is excellent and SUPER HELPFUL that he clears things up every class! The exams are mostly based on his lecture and papers are marked by TAs so nothing to do with him. Definitely would take him again.
